metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_671
metadata.comment:
Rationale: The SV protein CSPalpha is not essential for synaptic transmission, but acts as a chaperone for SNAP-25 to regulate the synaptic vesicle cycle (i.e. SV recycling and SV priming).

CSPalpha KO NMJ nerve terminals were analyzed by electrophysiology (Fig. 1) and they exhibit a reduction in the RRP due to a defect in vesicle priming (Fig. 1H) - probably due to a loss of SNAP-25 (Fig. 2). Measurements of Synapto-pHluorin (SpH) fluorescence in response to high-frequency trains of action potentials reveals an impairment of vesicle recycling/endocytosis in CSPalpha KO terminals (Fig.4-6).. Experimental description: Littermate offspring obtained from matings of the CSP-α-KO-spHtg line was used for experiments.
